text: fat is 54g. omega3 is where things start to fall off. for nuts, fat quality has to justify the calorie density.
carbs is 20g. the fibre support is doing a lot of the work. for a nut, the score starts with fat chemistry and then asks what else it brings.
protein is 17g. protein quality is a real strength here. protein is support here, not the whole argument.
vitamin e at 20% DV stand out most. that is only mild support overall.
magnesium at 32% DV, zinc at 18% DV, iron at 18% DV stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.

on the plus side. portable, practical snack food because nuts score real points in life because they are easy to use. fat and texture help satiety because they can hold up better than many snack foods. still a recognisable whole-food option because that keeps them cleaner than most processed snacks.

on the weaker side. calorie density is very high because that makes portion control a real issue. protein is useful but not elite because nuts help, but they are not protein stars. cost or allergy issues can limit use because practical downsides still count.
C tier. Best only in small amounts when the calories stay under control.
